name: ISO/IEC 25019:2023 — Quality-in-use model (SQuaRE)
summary: The SQuaRE quality-in-use model: the outcomes of using a product in a specified context of use, decomposed into effectiveness, efficiency, satisfaction, freedom from risk and context coverage. Anchors the quality-in-use coverage domain — whether requirements state the user/stakeholder outcomes the system must achieve, not just its features.

leaves.name: Effectiveness
leaves.description: Accuracy and completeness with which users achieve specified goals in the intended context of use.

leaves.name: Efficiency
leaves.description: Resources (time, effort, cost) expended in relation to the accuracy and completeness with which users achieve goals.

leaves.name: Satisfaction
leaves.description: Degree to which user needs are met (usefulness, trust, pleasure, comfort) when using the product.

leaves.name: Freedom from risk
leaves.description: Mitigation of potential risks to the user or stakeholders — economic, health and safety, and environmental.

leaves.name: Context coverage
leaves.description: Extent to which quality-in-use is achieved across the intended contexts (context completeness) and beyond them (flexibility).
